name: observe-whatsapp
description: Observe and troubleshoot WhatsApp in Kapso: debug message delivery, inspect webhook deliveries/retries, triage API errors, and run health checks. Use when investigating production issues, message failures, or webhook delivery problems.
capabilities: List and inspect WhatsApp messages by phone number ID or message ID
Debug webhook delivery attempts and retry patterns
Triage message errors and API logs
Run health checks on phone numbers and projects
Explore OpenAPI specifications for Kapso platform endpoints
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md). Static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks as shell commands, but all 28 external_commands findings are false positives from code formatting. Network and env_access findings reference YAML frontmatter URLs and documented environment variable requirements, not actual code execution. No executable code exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Troubleshoot WhatsApp production issues efficiently. This skill provides commands to inspect message delivery, debug webhook failures, and run health checks on the Kapso platform.
userTitle: Debug WhatsApp message delivery and webhook issues
limitations: Requires Kapso API credentials (KAPSO_API_BASE_URL and KAPSO_API_KEY) to be configured
Only works with Kapso platform, not direct WhatsApp Business API
Cannot create, update, or delete webhooks (use integrate-whatsapp skill for that)
Requires Node.js environment to execute the diagnostic scripts

useCases.title: Production incident response
useCases.description: Quickly investigate why WhatsApp messages are not being delivered to customers during an active incident.

useCases.title: Webhook debugging
useCases.description: Inspect webhook delivery attempts to understand why incoming messages or status updates are not being received.

useCases.title: Health monitoring
useCases.description: Run periodic health checks on phone numbers and projects to proactively detect issues before they impact users.

promptTemplates.title: Basic message lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to check if a WhatsApp message was delivered. The phone number is +1234567890. Help me find the conversation and check its delivery status.
promptTemplates.scenario: Customer reports not receiving a message

promptTemplates.title: Webhook delivery investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: Our webhook endpoint stopped receiving events from WhatsApp. Use the webhook-deliveries script to check recent delivery attempts and identify any error patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: Incoming messages not reaching your application

promptTemplates.title: Error triage workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: We're seeing increased message errors in production. Run through the full error triage: check message errors, review API logs, and identify any webhook delivery failures. Summarize the root cause.

promptTemplates.title: Health check automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a health check on phone number ID <id> and generate a status report. Include any failed health indicators and recommended remediation steps.

bestPractices: Always check message errors and API logs together to correlate failures with root causes
Run health checks proactively before deploying changes to WhatsApp integrations
Use the lookup-conversation script to get full context when investigating individual message issues
antiPatterns: Do not run diagnostic scripts repeatedly in quick succession, as this may rate-limit API access
Do not use this skill for webhook setup or configuration changes
Avoid investigating issues without first verifying that API credentials are valid and not expired

faq.question: What credentials do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need KAPSO_API_BASE_URL (the host URL without /platform/v1 path) and KAPSO_API_KEY environment variables configured.

faq.question: Can this skill create or modify WhatsApp webhooks?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for observation and debugging only. Use the integrate-whatsapp skill for webhook setup, updates, or deletion.

faq.question: Why are some commands showing npm install requirements?
faq.answer: The scripts depend on Node.js packages. Run npm install once before using the diagnostic scripts to install required dependencies.

faq.question: What does the openapi-explore.mjs script do?
faq.answer: It allows you to search and explore the Kapso platform OpenAPI specification, helping you find available endpoints, operations, and schemas.

faq.question: How do I check the health of a specific phone number?
faq.answer: Use the whatsapp-health.js script with the --phone-number-id flag followed by the phone number ID you want to check.

faq.question: What should I do if webhook deliveries are failing?
faq.answer: First check your webhook server is responding with 2xx status codes. Then review webhook-deliveries.js output for retry patterns and error codes. Verify your webhook signature validation is correct.
